title: ActivityWatch vs Timely for Solo users
meta_description: ActivityWatch vs Timely for Solo users: ActivityWatch wins for local tracking, while Timely requires cloud syncing and accounts.
constraint_lens: This person wants time tracking that runs locally without relying on accounts or cloud syncing.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for Solo users who want fully local time tracking without cloud accounts.
one_second_verdict.reason: Timely fails first because it requires syncing data to cloud services and maintaining an online account.

verdict.summary: ActivityWatch is the better choice when you want private time tracking that runs entirely on your device. It stores all activity data locally and does not require an account or cloud syncing. Timely relies on cloud services and account-based access, which adds dependency and ongoing connection requirements.
verdict.decision_rule: If tracking time requires syncing data to cloud services and maintaining an online account, Timely fails first.

sections.heading: Why ActivityWatch fits Solo users better
sections.content: This solo user wants full control over their data without relying on external services. ActivityWatch fits because it runs locally and stores all tracking data on your device. That removes the need to manage accounts or depend on cloud syncing.

bullets.point: ActivityWatch stores all tracking data locally on your device without cloud syncing.
bullets.why_it_matters: This keeps your data private and removes reliance on external services.

bullets.point: The tool runs without requiring account creation or login.
bullets.why_it_matters: This eliminates setup steps and ongoing account management.

bullets.point: Activity tracking happens continuously on device without sending data externally.
bullets.why_it_matters: This ensures full control over your tracking data.

bullets.point: Timely stores and syncs time tracking data through cloud based accounts.
bullets.why_it_matters: This enables access across devices, but requires ongoing account use.

bullets.point: Time entries and activity data are managed through an online dashboard.
bullets.why_it_matters: This centralizes data, but depends on internet connectivity.

bullets.point: Automatic tracking relies on cloud processing and syncing of activity data.
bullets.why_it_matters: This adds convenience, but introduces external dependency.

items.fails_when: ActivityWatch becomes limiting when you need multi device syncing or shared access across teams.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Timely if you need cloud based syncing and collaboration.

items.fails_when: Timely breaks down when you want fully local tracking but must rely on cloud syncing and account access.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use ActivityWatch if you want private on device tracking.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This could flip if you need to access your time tracking data across multiple devices or share it with others. In that case, Timely may be more useful despite the cloud dependency.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Pick ActivityWatch if you want local only time tracking.
Pick Timely if you need cloud syncing and access across devices.
Avoid Timely if you do not want to rely on accounts or internet connectivity.

faqs.q: Why is ActivityWatch better for solo users?
faqs.a: Because it stores all tracking data locally without requiring accounts or cloud syncing.

faqs.q: What makes Timely less suitable here?
faqs.a: It depends on cloud syncing and account access for tracking data.
No. It is useful for users who want cloud based access and syncing.

faqs.q: When should I choose Timely instead?
faqs.a: Choose Timely when you need to access your data across devices or share it with others.
